subtitle: Semantic Web Applications and Tools for Health Care and Life Sciences
hosted_by: Department of Medical Informatics, Amsterdam UMC
Advanced Compute & Data Core (ACDC), Amsterdam UMC

sessions.title: Registration & Welcome Coffee
Clinical Data Modeling with SULO
sessions.speaker: Remzi Celebi, Catalina Martinez-Costa, Michel Dumontier
sessions.abstract: Hands-on introduction to clinical data modeling using the Semantic Unified Language for Ontologies (SULO). Participants will learn to represent clinical knowledge using formal ontological patterns.

sessions.title: Making GraphRAG work for Healthcare and Life Sciences: Ensuring Knowledge Graph Quality for Grounded LLMs
sessions.speaker: Panos Alexopoulos
sessions.abstract: Practical workshop on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using knowledge graphs in healthcare and life sciences. Focus on knowledge graph quality criteria that ensure reliable LLM grounding.

sessions.title: Building Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Life Sciences with Ontop-VKG
sessions.speaker: Carsten Fortmann-Grote, Damien Goutte-Gattat, Rowdy de Groot, Tiago Lubiana, Joshua Moore, Daniel Olvera Cabrera, Andra Waagmeester
sessions.affil: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Biology; German BioImaging / Konstanz University; Micelio / AmsterdamUMC
sessions.abstract: Learn to construct and query virtual knowledge graphs over relational and other data sources in life sciences using Ontop, without physically transforming the data.

sessions.title: Coffee Break
Lunch
Harmonising Provenance Information for Biomedical Data: the MIRAPIE Approach Applied to Health Research Data in EHDS Context
sessions.speaker: Kerstin Gierend, Alexander J Kellmann, Matthias Lobe, Vasundra Touré, Sabine Österle, Judith AH Wodke
sessions.affil: Heidelberg University Mannheim; University Medicine Greifswald; Universität Leipzig; SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics
sessions.abstract: Practical session on provenance harmonization for biomedical datasets using the MIRAPIE framework, applied to the European Health Data Space (EHDS) context.

sessions.title: Declarative Data Standardization to OMOP-CDM using LinkML
sessions.speaker: Alberto Labarga
sessions.abstract: Learn to map heterogeneous health datasets to the OMOP Common Data Model using declarative LinkML schemas, reducing manual transformation effort.

sessions.title: Learning SPARQL By Example
sessions.speaker: Vincent Emonet, Pauline Lubet, Tarcisio Mendes, Ana-Claudia Sima, Jerven Bolleman, Andra Waagmeester
sessions.affil: SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics; Micelio
sessions.abstract: Beginner-friendly introduction to SPARQL for querying RDF knowledge graphs in the life sciences and healthcare, with hands-on examples on real biomedical datasets.

sessions.title: Coffee Break
Registration & Welcome Coffee
Welcome to SWAT4HCLS 2026
sessions.speaker: Sabine Österle

sessions.title: Looking in and through the looking-glass
sessions.speaker: Prof. Ronald Cornet
sessions.abstract: Reflects on 25 years researching semantics and ontologies for healthcare data representation, addressing technical, social, and governance aspects while examining progress, pitfalls, and future directions in semantic interoperability for reusable health data.
sessions.bio: Full Professor of Medical Informatics at Amsterdam UMC. His work focuses on semantic interoperability and information modeling, with consistent emphasis on making clinical and biomedical data more FAIR.

sessions.title: Inductive Link Prediction for Missing Medical Codes in Personal Health Knowledge Graphs
Enabling Semantic Traceability in Health Data: The Health-RI Semantic Interoperability Initiative
Coffee Break
Semantic Interoperability at National Scale: The SPHN Federated Clinical Routine Dataset
sessions.speaker: Jan Armida, Vasundra Touré, Philip Krauss, Deepak Unni, Harald Witte, Davide Chiarugi, and co-authors

sessions.title: Efficient Querying of Federated Large-Scale Clinical RDF Knowledge Graphs in the Swiss Personalized Health Network
Scholia 2026: Compliance with SPARQL 1.1
sessions.speaker: Egon Willighagen, Daniel Mietchen, Peter Patel-Schneider, Konrad Linden, Lars Willighagen, Wolfgang Fahl

sessions.title: Lunch
Semantic Knowledge Graphs for Drug Repurposing and Precision Medicine in Complex Neuropsychiatric Disorders
sessions.speaker: Samaneh Jozashoori, Nirmal Kannaiyan
sessions.abstract: An end-to-end workflow combining large-scale biomedical knowledge graphs with omics data and functional assays to support drug repurposing for neuropsychiatric patient groups with specific genetic predisposition. Presented by metaphacts and Systasy.

sessions.title: KIK-V implements a nationwide federative network for accountability information about business operations and quality in the elderly care
sessions.speaker: Reinier Morra, Victor den Bak, René Houwen
sessions.abstract: KIK-V implements a nationwide federative network for accountability information about business operations and quality in the elderly care, using SPARQL queries and an ontology-based model. About 35 elderly care providers are already operational.

sessions.title: Poster Flash Presentations
sessions.abstract: Short flash presentations by all poster and demo authors to introduce their work before the poster session.

sessions.title: Poster Session & Coffee Break
sessions.abstract: Presentations of all accepted posters and demonstrations. Coffee served. Sponsor engagement.

sessions.title: Modular composition of SPARQL queries for focusing on what to look for rather than how to get it
FDPcrawleR: A Lightweight R Framework for Auditing FAIR Data Points and FAIR Virtual Platforms
sessions.speaker: Kristina Vodorezova, Alberto Cámara, Nirupama Benis, Andra Waagmeester, Mark D. Wilkinson, Ronald Cornet

sessions.title: Keynote — Prof. Hannah Bast
sessions.speaker: Prof. Hannah Bast
sessions.abstract: Talk title to be announced. Prof. Bast specialises in algorithms, information retrieval, and high-performance systems for knowledge graph search, bridging rigorous computer science with real-world applications.
sessions.bio: Professor at University of Freiburg specialising in algorithms, information retrieval, and high-performance systems for RDF and SPARQL use cases.

sessions.title: Conference Dinner
sessions.abstract: Social dinner included with registration. A great opportunity to connect with fellow attendees over food and drinks.

sessions.title: Registration & Welcome Coffee
Knowledge alignment for LLMs: A strategy for reproducibility?
sessions.speaker: Prof. Janna Hastings
sessions.abstract: Discusses combining large language models with formal ontologies and logical constraints to improve reliability and ensure reproducibility, with examples from chemistry and behavioural science.
sessions.bio: Previously at EBI and University of Cambridge. From January 2026, leads the Human-Centred Health AI group at Idiap, focusing on improving AI approaches for evidence and decision-making.

sessions.title: A Standards-Based Knowledge Graph that Bridges Scientific Workflows, Run-Time Provenance, and Tool Registries
sessions.speaker: Marie Schmit, Ulysse Le Clanche, George Marchment, Sarah Cohen-Boulakia, Olivier Dameron, Alban Gaignard, Frédéric Lemoine, Hervé Ménager

sessions.title: Scaling up data driven research can leverage the EHDS but needs better quality and more semantically interoperable data
sessions.speaker: Prof. Dipak Kalra
sessions.affil: European Institute for Innovation through Health Data (i~HD)
sessions.abstract: Examines how the European Health Data Space accelerates real-world data availability while highlighting critical success factors including data provenance, quality, standards, and public trust.
sessions.bio: President of i~HD. Champions approaches combining technical interoperability with ethical, legal, and organisational trust to support responsible data sharing across healthcare and research.

sessions.title: Panel discussion: Semantic web and life sciences
sessions.speaker: Andrea Splendiani

sessions.title: Registration
SWAT4HCLS Biohackathon Co-working Space
sessions.speaker: Andra Waagmeester
sessions.abstract:
Full-day biohackathon co-working. Four active projects:
1. From DCAT to HealthDCAT-AP: Comparative Analysis of National Healthcare Metadata Catalog Schemas to Enable Federated Interoperability in Europe
2. OpenMRS as a Living Lab: Linking EHRs to the Semantic Web for Personalized Medicine
3. Bring Your Concepts: Creating Reusable Meaning Mappings for Health Data Interoperability
4. Microscopy Metadata Meets Semantic Web

biohackathon.note: Parallel hacking room available March 23–25. Main event March 26.

projects.title: From DCAT to HealthDCAT-AP
projects.description: Comparative analysis of healthcare metadata catalogue schemas across Europe. Participants will analyse similarities and differences among national DCAT-AP and HealthDCAT-AP implementations to create harmonisation recommendations.

projects.title: OpenMRS as a Living Lab
projects.leads: Claude Nanjo, Stephanie Medlock, Ronald Cornet, Andra Waagmeester (Amsterdam UMC)
projects.description: Uses open-source OpenMRS to demonstrate integration with Semantic Web knowledge sources for enriching clinical data with linked biomedical knowledge, focusing on privacy-preserving approaches and reproducible patterns for decision support.

projects.title: Bring Your Concepts
projects.leads: Pedro Paulo F. Barcelos et al. (Health-RI)
projects.description: Hands-on mapping clinic aligning concepts from ontologies and vocabularies to a shared semantic hub, operationalising the Health-RI Semantic Interoperability Initiative framework.

projects.title: Microscopy Metadata Meets Semantic Web
projects.description: Focuses on the intersection of Semantic Web technologies and the microscopy ecosystem, including OMERO platform, OME-NGFF standard, and bioimaging repositories for strengthened interoperability.

travel_alert: ⚠️ Extensive road and rail maintenance is scheduled throughout 2026 in Amsterdam. Check live travel conditions before setting off.

from_anywhere.label: From Schiphol Airport
steps: Take NS Sprinter (platform -1) direct to Amsterdam Holendrecht — no changes needed.
Journey time: ~25 min. Single ticket from €5.50 (OVpay: just tap your bank card).
Exit Holendrecht station, cross the station square and walk over the bridge — the AMC main entrance is visible directly ahead. (~5 min walk)

from_anywhere.label: From Amsterdam Centraal
steps: Metro line 50 (direction Gein or Gaasperplas) from Amsterdam Centraal → exit at Amsterdam Holendrecht. Journey ~35 min.
OR: NS Sprinter from Amsterdam Centraal to Amsterdam Holendrecht. Journey ~28 min.
Exit station, cross the square and walk over the bridge to the AMC main entrance. (~5 min walk)

from_anywhere.label: By bus
steps: Bus lines 120, 276, 370, 41, 47 and night bus N85 all stop near AmsterdamUMC, location AMC.
Nearest stops: Holendrechtplein, Amsterdam Meibergdreef, Tafelbergweg.
Use 9292.nl or Moovit to plan from your starting point.

from_anywhere.label: By car
steps: Signposted from Amsterdam ring road A10 (exit S112), and from A2 and A9.
Paid parking available on site.
⚠️ Allow extra travel time — extensive road maintenance throughout 2026. Check Waze or Google Maps for live conditions.

from_anywhere.label: By bicycle
steps: Cycle via the new park along Meibergdreef.
Free covered bike parking available beneath the stairs opposite the main entrance.
Use CyclOSM for a cycling-optimised map.

indoor_nav.title: Inside AmsterdamUMC, location AMC
indoor_nav.address_full:
AmsterdamUMC, location AMC
Meibergdreef 9
1105 AZ Amsterdam-Zuidoost
Netherlands
indoor_nav.conference_rooms: All sessions take place in or around Collegezaal 4 (Lecture room 4), building G — accessible from Annaplein. The foyer of Collegezaal 4 hosts registration, coffee breaks, and poster sessions. Biohackathon: Collegezaal 3 (Lecture room 3), building J.

rooms.name: Collegezaal 4
rooms.note: Central building, directly on Annaplein. Follow signs to building G from the Poliplein.

rooms.name: Collegezaal 3
rooms.note: Upper part of building J, next to Collegezaal 2. Use entrance 2 (Ingang Oost / P4) to reach it more directly.

rooms.name: Collegezaal 2
rooms.note: Adjacent to Collegezaal 3 in building J. Use entrance 2 (Ingang Oost / P4) for the shortest route.

entrances.name: Hoofdingang AMC
entrances.location: South side, Meibergdreef 9 — directly opposite the bridge from Holendrecht station (P1 parking)

entrances.name: Ingang Oost (faculteit)
entrances.location: West side of campus (confusingly labelled Oost on maps) — nearest to P4 parking and building J

entrances.name: Ingang West (P2)
entrances.location: East side, P2 parking — leads toward buildings H and M

nav_sections.title: Arriving at AmsterdamUMC, location AMC
steps: The easiest access is via the Hoofdingang (Main Entrance, P1) of AmsterdamUMC, location AMC.
Public transport users arrive via Station Amsterdam Holendrecht — exit the station and walk across the bridge; the main entrance is directly ahead (~5 min walk).
By car: enter via P1 (visitor parking, 34 spaces) or P2 (paid parking). P1 is right next to the Hoofdingang.

nav_sections.title: From the main entrance to the Foyer of Collegezaal 4
steps: Enter through the Hoofdingang / Main entrance (entrance ①).
After entering the hospital, follow the internal corridor toward Annaplein — the central square inside the building.
From Annaplein, continue toward building G, which leads to the main conference area.
The Foyer of Collegezaal 4 (Lecture room 4) is directly accessible from Annaplein in building G. Look for SWAT4HCLS signage.

registration.location: Foyer of Collegezaal 4 (Lecture room 4) — building G, near Annaplein
registration.note: The foyer directly outside the lecture hall hosts the registration desk, coffee breaks, and poster sessions throughout the conference.

steps_from_entrance: Enter via the Hoofdingang / Main entrance (entrance ①, south side — straight ahead from Holendrecht bridge).
Follow the internal corridor toward Annaplein — the central square inside the building.
From Annaplein, continue toward building G.
The Foyer of Collegezaal 4 (Lecture room 4) is in building G, directly on Annaplein — follow SWAT4HCLS signs.
tips: The AMC is a large teaching hospital — allow 5–10 extra minutes to find your room on Day 1.
All three conference rooms (Collegezalen 2, 3 and 4) are connected via the internal hospital street — you can walk between them without going outside.
The foyer of Collegezaal 4 (building G, Annaplein) is used for registration, coffee breaks, and posters during the main conference days.
Toilets and the hospital canteen are available throughout the building, including near Annaplein and Poliplein.
The 'Route in Amsterdam UMC' app uses augmented reality to guide you to any room — download it before arriving.
Accessibility: the building is fully wheelchair accessible. Shuttle service operates 09:00–17:00 from the main entrance (call 06-20360622).
A hospital-wide shuttle runs 09:00–17:00 between Hoofdingang, Poliplein (A0), and H0 reception — helpful if walking is difficult.

transport.label: Nearest station
transport.detail: Amsterdam Holendrecht — served by Metro 50, Metro 54, and NS Sprinter. Exit station, cross the square and walk over the bridge (~5 min) — the AMC main entrance is visible directly ahead.

transport.label: From Amsterdam Centraal
transport.detail: Metro 50 (direction Gein/Gaasperplas) → Holendrecht (~35 min). Or NS Sprinter → Holendrecht (~28 min).

transport.label: From Schiphol Airport
transport.detail: NS Sprinter direct to Amsterdam Holendrecht (~25 min, from €5.50 OVpay). Walk 5 min to AMC entrance.

transport.label: By bicycle
transport.detail: Via the park along Meibergdreef. Free covered bike parking opposite the main entrance.

transport.label: By car
transport.detail: Signposted from A10 (exit S112), A2, and A9. Paid parking on site. ⚠️ Allow extra time — 2026 road works.

tickets.name: OVpay (contactless card/phone)
tickets.note: Tap in/out with bank card. Daily GVB cap €10.50.
